lead: This week, innovation in AI and emerging technologies takes center stage with significant developments from major companies and startups alike, particularly in the Australian tech ecosystem.

stories.title: Apple reportedly testing out four different styles for its smart glasses
stories.summary: Apple is reportedly exploring four distinct designs for its upcoming smart glasses, aiming to compete with Meta's Ray-Ban models. The designs range from classic rectangular frames to more modern oval shapes, with various colors being tested. The glasses are expected to integrate seamlessly with the Apple ecosystem, enhancing user experience with features like photo and video capture.

stories.title: Anthropic brings Claude into Microsoft Word, and legal contract review leads its use cases
stories.summary: Anthropic has introduced a beta add-in that integrates its AI, Claude, directly into Microsoft Word. This feature allows users to see AI-generated edits as tracked changes, with a focus on legal contract review among its initial applications. This move marks a significant step in AI's integration into productivity tools, enhancing efficiency for users.

stories.title: OpenAI’s new $100 ChatGPT Pro plan targets Claude Max with five times the Codex access
stories.summary: OpenAI has launched a new ChatGPT Pro plan priced at $100 per month, aimed at competing directly with Anthropic's Claude Max. This plan offers users five times more access to Codex, enhancing capabilities for developers and businesses. This strategic pricing and feature enhancement reflects the competitive landscape in AI services.

stories.title: NDIS Commission to have a new intelligent risk engine by August
stories.summary: The NDIS Commission in Australia is set to implement a new intelligent risk engine by August, coinciding with ongoing budget scrutiny. This technology aims to enhance the management of risks associated with the National Disability Insurance Scheme, showcasing innovation in public sector technology.

stories.title: The Netherlands becomes the first European country to approve Tesla’s FSD Supervised
stories.summary: The Netherlands has become the first European country to approve Tesla's Full Self-Driving (Supervised) software. This approval follows extensive testing and marks a significant milestone in the adoption of autonomous vehicle technology in Europe, setting a precedent for other countries.

stories.title: X says it’s reducing payments to clickbait accounts
stories.summary: X has announced a reduction in payments to accounts that excessively share clickbait content, aiming to improve the quality of information on its platform. This move reflects a broader trend in social media to prioritize meaningful engagement over sensationalism.

stories.title: Apple reportedly testing AI Glasses in Several Frame Styles
stories.summary: In addition to its smart glasses, Apple is reportedly testing various styles for AI glasses that could enhance user interaction with augmented reality. The inclusion of advanced camera technology and integration with existing Apple services could position these glasses as a key player in the wearable tech market.

stories.title: OpenAI says Elon Musk is orchestrating a last-minute 'legal ambush' before trial
stories.summary: OpenAI has accused Elon Musk of attempting to disrupt ongoing legal proceedings with last-minute changes to his lawsuit. This high-profile case highlights the tensions between Musk and OpenAI, particularly over the direction and governance of AI development.

stories.title: Rockstar Games says hack will have ‘no impact’
stories.summary: Rockstar Games has confirmed that a recent data breach affecting a third-party provider will not impact its operations or players. The company has stated that the compromised data was limited, emphasizing its commitment to security in the gaming industry.

stories.title: Today's Wordle Hints, Answer and Help for April 13, #1759
stories.summary: As the popularity of word games continues to rise, today's Wordle hints and answers provide a fun distraction for players. This ongoing engagement with puzzle games demonstrates the intersection of technology and entertainment in everyday life.
